title: Advanced Dynamic Programming and State Compression
description: Scale sequence alignment and constrained-selection models beyond naive tables. Learners cumulatively produce an optimized DP with equivalence evidence through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Model longest-common-subsequence and edit-distance choices with boundary rows and reconstruction.
Distinguish zero-one, unbounded, subset-sum, and value-indexed knapsack states.
Compress DP rows only when overwritten states are no longer needed.
